Package minum.logging

Class Logger

java.lang.Object
minum.logging.Logger
All Implemented Interfaces:
ILogger
Direct Known Subclasses:
TestLogger
public class Logger extends Object implements ILogger
Implementation of ILogger

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected final LoggingActionQueue
    loggerPrinter
    The ActionQueue that handles all our messages thread-safely by taking them off the top of a queue.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Logger(LoggingContext context)
    This constructor initializes an ActionQueue to handle log messages.
    Logger(LoggingContext context, String name)
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static List<LoggingLevel>
    convertLoggingStringsToEnums(List<String> logLevels)
    Given a list of strings representing logging levels, convert it to a list of enums.
    void
    logAsyncError(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
    Use this particularly for those cases where we want to log an error but we're stuck inside an asynchronous piece of code, for example, anything unexpectedly bad that happens in ActionQueue
    void
    logAudit(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
    This is for logging business-related topics
    void
    logDebug(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
    For minum.logging data that is helpful to debugging a running system.
    void
    logHelper(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg, LoggingLevel loggingLevel)
    A helper method to reduce duplication
    void
    logTrace(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
    this is similar to ILogger.logDebug(ThrowingSupplier) except that it gets run *A LOT*, which can spam up the logs.
    static ILogger
    make(Constants constants)
    Build a logger.
    static void
    printf(String msg, Object... args)
     
    static String
    showWhiteSpace(String msg)
    Given a string that may have whitespace chars, render it in a way we can see
    void
    stop()
    The logger has to stand apart from the rest of the system, or else we'll have circular dependencies.

  • Field Details

    • loggerPrinter

      protected final LoggingActionQueue loggerPrinter
      The ActionQueue that handles all our messages thread-safely by taking them off the top of a queue.

  • Constructor Details

    • Logger

      public Logger(LoggingContext context)
      This constructor initializes an ActionQueue to handle log messages.

    • Logger

      public Logger(LoggingContext context, String name)

  • Method Details

    • make

      public static ILogger make(Constants constants)
      Build a logger.

      Some interesting aspects of this builder:

      • It uses its own ExecutorService, separate from the one for the rest of the system.
      • It uses its own Context object, separate from the regular Context

    • convertLoggingStringsToEnums

      public static List<LoggingLevel> convertLoggingStringsToEnums(List<String> logLevels)
      Given a list of strings representing logging levels, convert it to a list of enums. Log levels are enumerated in LoggingLevel.

    • logDebug

      public void logDebug(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
      Description copied from interface: ILogger
      For minum.logging data that is helpful to debugging a running system.
      Specified by:
      logDebug in interface ILogger
      Parameters:
      msg - example: () -> "Hello"

    • logTrace

      public void logTrace(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
      Description copied from interface: ILogger
      this is similar to ILogger.logDebug(ThrowingSupplier) except that it gets run *A LOT*, which can spam up the logs. Not to mention, that can have performance impacts. By default, we won't show trace information unless requested.
      Specified by:
      logTrace in interface ILogger
      Parameters:
      msg - example: () -> "Hello"

    • logAudit

      public void logAudit(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
      Description copied from interface: ILogger
      This is for logging business-related topics
      Specified by:
      logAudit in interface ILogger

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Description copied from interface: ILogger
      The logger has to stand apart from the rest of the system, or else we'll have circular dependencies. For that reason, when we are shutting down the system it is necessary to explicitly stop the logger.
      Specified by:
      stop in interface ILogger

    • logAsyncError

      public void logAsyncError(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg)
      Description copied from interface: ILogger
      Use this particularly for those cases where we want to log an error but we're stuck inside an asynchronous piece of code, for example, anything unexpectedly bad that happens in ActionQueue
      Specified by:
      logAsyncError in interface ILogger
      Parameters:
      msg - example: () -> "Hello"

    • logHelper

      public void logHelper(ThrowingSupplier<String,Exception> msg, LoggingLevel loggingLevel)
      A helper method to reduce duplication

    • showWhiteSpace

      public static String showWhiteSpace(String msg)
      Given a string that may have whitespace chars, render it in a way we can see

    • printf

      public static void printf(String msg, Object... args)
